personas.name: Referral Queen
personas.summary: 10+ years, referral-led, impatient, ROI-sensitive, not very tech savvy.

personas.name: Control Veteran
personas.summary: 20+ years, cares about accuracy, brand control, and human oversight.

personas.name: Busy Lease Specialist
personas.summary: Paperwork-heavy, time-poor, wants the shortest possible path.

personas.name: Luxury Brand Keeper
personas.summary: High-end listing agent, very sensitive to tone, pacing, fonts, and cheapness.

personas.name: Skeptical Brokerage Switcher
personas.summary: Recently trained on a brokerage tool, cautious, compares everything to existing systems.

scenarios.title: Referral entry, direct photo send
success_signals: Directly acknowledges the photos
Does not force feature education first
Keeps the next action short
failure_signals: Long help text before action
Too many choices before first value
No hint what happens next

scenarios.title: Referral entry, user asks if this is an app
scenarios.user_message: Is this an app? How do I use this?
success_signals: Answers the confusion directly
Gives one clear next step
Does not dump every capability
failure_signals: Generic menu-style answer
Still unclear what to do next
Too much product explanation

scenarios.title: Referral entry, user asks about security
scenarios.user_message: How do I know this is secure and not spam?
success_signals: Responds to trust directly
Avoids sounding defensive
Still gives a simple next step
failure_signals: Dodges the security concern
Gets overly technical
Leaves the user with no action

scenarios.title: Landing page, user fills form before trying
success_signals: Form feels short and justified
User understands why the form exists
A clear post-form next step exists
failure_signals: Feels like setup before value
User wonders why trial is blocked by form
No obvious next action after submit

scenarios.title: Landing page, user wants to try video immediately
success_signals: Primary CTA clearly points to first trial
The page does not force the user to choose too much
The first trial feels low-commitment
failure_signals: Competing CTAs confuse the user
The page teaches instead of activating
The user still asks what to do

scenarios.title: Landing page, user wants to try daily insight first
success_signals: The page makes it clear that insight-first is allowed
Trying insight does not feel like a dead-end
A natural handoff to video exists
failure_signals: The page implies video is the only valid first use
Insight looks like a side feature with no path onward
The user hesitates between two equal CTAs

scenarios.title: Operator interview leads to starter tasks
success_signals: The interview feels helpful, not interrogative
The next tasks are short and concrete
The task order clearly matches the user's context
failure_signals: The interview creates more cognitive load
The tasks are vague or too many
The user still does not know where to start

scenarios.title: Natural traffic, new user asks for daily insight
success_signals: Allows the new user into the insight path immediately
Avoids kicking them back to generic welcome
Keeps the action clear
failure_signals: Forces onboarding before any value
Sends a generic welcome instead of continuing
Does not clarify what will happen next

scenarios.title: Natural traffic, new user sends photos with no context
success_signals: Clearly acknowledges the photos
Keeps the next request minimal
Does not demand feature understanding first
failure_signals: Over-explains the product
Requests too many fields immediately
No visible progress toward first value

scenarios.title: Natural traffic, user asks price before trying
scenarios.user_message: How much is this per month?
success_signals: Treats the ROI question as normal
Does not become evasive
Still preserves a trial next step
failure_signals: Avoids pricing entirely
Pushes the user into a trial without answering
Sounds like a sales script

scenarios.title: Landing page skim, user does not act
success_signals: The page makes the first move obvious
The first move feels low-risk
Value proposition is clear without over-explaining
failure_signals: Too much product information before action
No clear primary CTA
Video and insight feel equally vague

scenarios.title: User receives onboarding form but resists filling it
success_signals: User understands the value of filling the form
There is a fallback path to try before filling everything
The form feels short and relevant
failure_signals: Form looks like work before value
The user cannot bypass it for a quick trial
The submit confirmation does not guide the next action

scenarios.title: Video-first path with missing address and price
success_signals: Missing data is requested in one compact turn
The agent still sees momentum toward a video
The request feels necessary, not bureaucratic
failure_signals: Asks too many follow-up questions
Loses the sense of progress
Reads like form filling

scenarios.title: Should sample precede preference collection
success_signals: A sample reduces hesitation without creating a detour
Preference collection happens only when useful
The path still feels short
failure_signals: Sample path delays real value
Preference collection feels premature
The user does not know when the real trial starts

scenarios.title: Producing wait rhythm and message tone
success_signals: The wait feels active, not silent
Progress feels human and useful
The user knows roughly what is happening
failure_signals: Long silence
Message sounds like an internal log
The user wonders if the job is stuck

scenarios.title: Delivery format, caption, hashtags, and next action
success_signals: The delivery feels polished and easy to scan
Caption and hashtags feel agent-authored
The next action is obvious
failure_signals: Caption looks AI-generated
Hashtags feel generic or bloated
The message is visually cluttered

scenarios.title: Revision phrasing and perceived control
success_signals: One natural-language change request feels enough
The user feels heard
The system explains what will change
failure_signals: Feels like filing a ticket
The user has to restate too much context
The system's updated output does not match the request

scenarios.title: Insight-first path for a user with no current listing
success_signals: The user can sample content with minimal setup
The insight looks immediately postable
The path keeps the door open for video later
failure_signals: Insight feels like secondary filler
The user still has to do too much setup
There is no conversion path to video

scenarios.title: Insight feels generic and not local
success_signals: The evaluator can clearly say why it feels local
The insight reflects the agent's market
The topic feels useful this week
failure_signals: The insight could belong to any city
The tone sounds generic
The user would not post it today

scenarios.title: Publish / skip / shorter / more professional controls
success_signals: The user can make a quick decision
The control language is natural
Skipping does not feel like failure
failure_signals: Too many decisions for a small piece of content
Controls are unclear or feel robotic
Shortening or tone changes feel unnatural

scenarios.title: Insight-first path converts into video request
success_signals: The handoff feels natural and helpful
The system uses what it learned in the insight flow
The user does not feel reset to zero
failure_signals: The video path ignores prior context
The handoff feels like starting over
The user is asked the same questions again

scenarios.title: Third-use path begins to compress
success_signals: Some questions disappear
The path feels more tailored
The user notices reduced friction
failure_signals: The path feels identical to the first use
Known preferences are ignored
No visible sense of progress

scenarios.title: Tenth-use path should not re-ask obvious preferences
success_signals: No re-asking of style, language, or obvious defaults
The user feels recognized
The system mentions what it already knows when useful
failure_signals: The user has to restate known preferences
The system feels stateless
No difference from new-user flow

scenarios.title: Twentieth-use path should feel like a trusted assistant
success_signals: The user would describe the product as a helper, not a tool
The product feels context-aware across video and insight
The user trusts it with less explanation
failure_signals: Still feels like a tool needing management
Context does not carry between flows
The user must think too much each time

scenarios.title: Twentieth-use referral willingness
success_signals: The evaluator can imagine recommending this to office peers
The strongest benefit is easy to explain
No major trust caveat remains
failure_signals: The evaluator says 'I'd use it, but not recommend it yet'
Too much explanation is needed before referral
Trust caveats still exist

scenarios.title: Dashboard supports initialization path management
success_signals: The operator can quickly tell who is video-ready vs insight-ready
The next action is inferable from the UI
Users needing follow-up are easy to spot
failure_signals: Readiness looks informative but not actionable
The dashboard does not expose path choice
Operators still need to open many profiles to triage

scenarios.title: Client detail helps decide the next best action
success_signals: Missing info is grouped by collection channel
The operator can decide whether to send form, ask in chat, or start a trial
The UI hints at the user's likely next path
failure_signals: The page is detailed but not decisive
Collection-source tags do not help action
The operator still cannot choose video vs insight vs interview

scenarios.title: Onboarding invite and form copy for low-patience agents
success_signals: The copy feels lightweight and credible
The benefit arrives before the burden
The user understands why the form exists
failure_signals: The copy feels tool-centric
The promise is vague
The form reads like setup work

scenarios.title: Skill brief editing is usable by non-technical operators
success_signals: Operators can understand what they are changing
Customization feels safe and scoped
The UI does not require engineering context
failure_signals: The skill editor looks too internal
Operators cannot predict the impact of changes
Customization becomes another burden
